agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive courier service with premium pricing, real-time tracking, and a refund policy for missed delivery windows. Synthesized from a multi-stakeholder product discovery workshop.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a specialized fulfillment mode within the broader Order/Delivery domain, touching Order Placement, Driver Assignment, Tracking, and Refunds.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option promising a 2-hour delivery window from order placement, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse, fulfilled by drivers with a minimum 4-star rating."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"A $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when the customer selects express delivery."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The 2-hour period starting from order placement within which the delivery is expected to be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to driver pickup at the warehouse. Exceeding this jeopardizes the delivery window."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge, separate from the express fee. Not refundable even when the express window is missed."},{"term":"Driver Rating Threshold","definition":"Minimum 4-star driver rating required to be eligible for express delivery assignments."},{"term":"Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute window to assign a replacement driver after a mid-delivery cancellation. Failure to reassign within this window triggers an automatic refund."},{"term":"Wrong Address Exception","definition":"A refund exclusion: if the customer provided an incorrect address, express fee refund is not issued regardless of delivery outcome."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"Legal's characterization of the express fee refund — a discretionary gesture, not a contractual obligation."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — exceeding 10km makes the 2-hour window operationally infeasible. Allowing orders outside this radius corrupts the service promise.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with a delivery address >10km from any warehouse, WHEN they attempt to select express delivery, THEN the express option must not be selectable."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operational constraint from Operations Lead — the 2-hour window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes. This is a hard physical/logistics constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order placed at time T, WHEN no driver has picked up by T+15min, THEN the system must escalate (trigger reassignment or alert operations)."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy that could be adjusted based on driver supply constraints, though relaxing it risks service quality.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a pool of available drivers, WHEN the system assigns a driver to an express order, THEN the assigned driver must have a rating >= 4.0 stars."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy subject to change by Finance or Product.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer selecting express delivery, WHEN the order total is calculated, THEN $12.99 is added as a separate express fee line item."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When the delivery window is missed, the express fee ($12.99) is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund policy, subject to business decision changes.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery completed after the 2-hour window, WHEN a refund is processed, THEN only the $12.99 express fee is refunded, not the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Express fee refunds are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational SLA for finance processing, adjustable.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a refund triggered for a missed express window, WHEN the refund is initiated, THEN it must be completed within 3 business days."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business exception policy — could be modified by customer success or legal.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a missed express window where the customer's address was flagged as incorrect, WHEN the refund eligibility is evaluated, THEN the refund must be denied."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or the express fee is automatically refunded.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Customer success policy. The 10-minute threshold and auto-refund are business decisions.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery where the driver cancels mid-route at time T, WHEN no replacement driver is assigned by T+10min, THEN the system must automatically trigger an express fee refund."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Express delivery customers must have real-time tracking of their assigned driver.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Real-time tracking is integral to the express delivery value proposition and operationally necessary for the time-sensitive nature of the service.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with an active express delivery, WHEN they open tracking, THEN they must see the driver's live location updating in real time."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse","when":"They view delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is displayed and selectable with the $12.99 fee shown"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer whose delivery address is more than 10km from any warehouse","when":"They view delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is hidden or disabled with an explanation"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"No driver picks up within 15 minutes","then":"The system escalates: attempts reassignment and alerts operations"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express delivery is completed after the 2-hour window","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"The $12.99 express fee is refunded and the base delivery fee is retained, processed within 3 business days"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express delivery missed its window but the customer provided an incorrect address","when":"Refund eligibility is evaluated","then":"The refund is denied and the customer is informed of the reason"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An express driver cancels mid-delivery","when":"10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned","then":"The express fee is automatically refunded to the customer"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An express driver cancels mid-delivery","when":"A replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes","then":"The delivery continues with the replacement driver and the 2-hour window remains in effect from original order time"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer has an active express delivery","when":"They open the tracking view","then":"They see the driver's real-time location on a map with live updates"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"No eligible drivers (4+ stars) available within 10km at order time","expected_behavior":"System should not accept the express order or should warn the customer of potential delays before charging the express fee. Needs product decision."},{"scenario":"Driver picks up at minute 14 but delivery completes within the 2-hour window","expected_behavior":"No refund — the delivery window was met. The 15-minute pickup SLA is an internal operational target, not a customer-facing refund trigger."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address after placing express order","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether the new address is validated against the 10km rule and whether this resets the delivery window. Needs product decision."},{"scenario":"Replacement driver also cancels","expected_behavior":"Should the 10-minute replacement clock reset? At what point does the system give up and auto-refund? Needs policy clarification."},{"scenario":"Express order placed 1 minute before warehouse closing","expected_behavior":"System should enforce warehouse operating hours as a precondition for express availability. Not addressed in any stakeholder input."},{"scenario":"Network/GPS outage prevents real-time tracking","expected_behavior":"Unclear if this constitutes a service failure triggering refund, or is a separate concern. Tracking loss doesn't mean delivery is late."},{"scenario":"Customer disputes 'wrong address' determination","expected_behavior":"Who adjudicates? What evidence is required? Needs dispute resolution process definition."},{"scenario":"2-hour window boundary — delivery arrives at exactly 2h 0m 0s","expected_behavior":"Is the window inclusive or exclusive? Needs precise definition (e.g., < 2h vs <= 2h)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"New revenue stream at $12.99 per express order. Refund liability is capped at the express fee only. Wrong-address exception reduces refund exposure. However, if the guarantee/best-effort contradiction isn't resolved, inconsistent refund processing could create unpredictable revenue leakage.","operational":"Requires real-time driver assignment engine with 15-minute pickup enforcement, 10-minute replacement SLA, driver rating filtering, and geo-fencing (10km radius). Significantly more complex than standard delivery operations. Operations must staff/route for sub-15-minute pickups.","user":"Premium experience: real-time tracking, fast delivery. But if marketing says 'guaranteed' and Legal says 'best effort,' customers will feel deceived when refunds are denied — major trust and retention risk.","risk":"HIGH — The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is a legal and brand risk. Marketing 'guaranteed 2 hours' while Legal disclaims it creates grounds for consumer protection complaints and class-action exposure in some jurisdictions. Must be resolved before launch.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Expect a guaranteed 2-hour delivery based on product messaging. If Legal's best-effort framing prevails, customers may feel misled when refunds are denied."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Higher performance bar (4-star minimum, 15-min pickup). Express assignments add time pressure and rating anxiety. May need incentive structure."},{"group":"Operations","impact":"Must build and maintain real-time assignment, geo-fencing, replacement routing, and escalation workflows. Significant new operational complexity."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Must support automated refund triggers (missed window, failed replacement) within 3 business days. Need refund reconciliation and wrong-address adjudication processes."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Directly affected by guarantee language. Must approve all customer-facing copy, terms of service, and refund policy language before launch."},{"group":"Customer Success","impact":"Will handle disputes around wrong-address exceptions, tracking issues, and unmet expectations. Need clear escalation playbooks."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"A direct contradiction exists between PM/Customer Success (who call this a 'guarantee' with prominent display) and Legal (who explicitly says it cannot be guaranteed in writing and refunds are goodwill only). This is not a nuance — it's a fundamental disagreement about the nature of the product promise that affects messaging, refund automation, and legal exposure. Until resolved, the product cannot be correctly specified.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99) is agreed by all parties","10km warehouse proximity requirement is clear","Driver 4-star rating threshold is clear","Base delivery fee is never refunded","Wrong-address exception to refunds is clear","3 business day refund processing window is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["How the 2-hour window start time is precisely defined (order placement vs. order confirmation vs. pickup)","Warehouse operating hours and express availability windows — never mentioned","Driver incentive/compensation for express — not discussed","What happens when no eligible drivers are available at order time","Dispute resolution process for wrong-address determinations","Whether the 10-minute replacement window resets on successive driver cancellations"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour delivery window starts at order placement time, not pickup time","The 10km radius is calculated as straight-line distance, not driving distance","Driver rating is based on overall historical rating, not recent-window rating","Only one warehouse needs to be within 10km, not all warehouses"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' (PM: 'guaranteed within 2 hours'; CS: 'The 2-hour guarantee should be shown prominently'). Legal explicitly states: 'We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing — this is a best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee. The refund is a goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These positions are mutually exclusive. You cannot prominently display a 'guarantee' while legally disclaiming it as best-effort. This affects: (1) all customer-facing copy and UI, (2) whether refunds are automated obligations or discretionary, (3) terms of service language, (4) potential regulatory/consumer-protection exposure.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Customer Success","Legal","Customers","Marketing"],"options":["A: Adopt Legal's position — label as 'best effort' with goodwill refund. Remove 'guarantee' from all messaging. Refund becomes discretionary.","B: Adopt PM/CS position — commit to guarantee with automatic refund. Legal must accept liability and draft enforceable terms.","C: Hybrid — market as 'Express Priority' without the word 'guarantee,' but implement automatic refunds as a published policy (not a legal guarantee). Refund is a policy commitment, not a contractual right."],"recommendation":"Option C. It threads the needle: customers get a reliable refund policy that builds trust, Legal avoids contractual guarantee language, and Product can market speed without legal exposure. The word 'guarantee' is removed from all copy; replaced with 'Express 2-Hour Delivery — or your express fee back.' This is a common pattern in logistics (Amazon, DoorDash) and is defensible. However, Legal must sign off on the final language."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success says refund should be automatic when replacement driver is not found within 10 minutes. Legal says refunds are goodwill gestures, not enforceable. If refunds are automated in the system (as CS demands), they become de facto policy — which contradicts Legal's position that they are discretionary. An automated system cannot exercise discretion.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Legal","Finance","Engineering"],"options":["A: Make all refunds manual/discretionary — CS agents evaluate each case.","B: Automate refunds per CS requirements, but Legal accepts this as published policy.","C: Automate refunds by default with a manual override for exception cases (e.g., wrong address, fraud)."],"recommendation":"Option C. Automate the happy path (missed window → refund), build in exception handling for wrong-address and fraud cases. This satisfies CS's customer experience goals while giving Legal the discretion escape valve for edge cases."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"The product cannot launch with both 'guaranteed 2-hour delivery' messaging and a legal disclaimer that it is 'best effort.' These are contradictory promises that will confuse customers, expose the company to consumer protection risk, and make refund automation impossible to specify correctly.","business_rule_broken":"The nature of the 2-hour commitment (BR-5, BR-8) cannot be implemented until stakeholders agree whether it is a guarantee or best-effort."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive courier service with premium pricing, real-time tracking, and a refund policy for missed delivery windows. Synthesized from a multi-stakeholder product discovery workshop.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a specialized fulfillment mode within the broader Order/Delivery domain, touching Order Placement, Driver Assignment, Tracking, and Refunds.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","d

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these workshop notes carefully for contradictions and gaps.

Key contradiction: PM and Customer Success say "guaranteed within 2 hours" but Legal says it's "best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee." This is a direct contradiction about the nature of the commitment.

Let me also look for other issues...

PM says "guaranteed" - Legal says "best effort" - this is a clear contradiction.

Other observations:
- The refund trigger conditions have some ambiguity: PM says refund on missed window, Finance says no refund for wrong address, CS says auto-refund if replacement not found in 10 minutes. 

The core issue here is that the 2-hour guarantee language CS wants displayed prominently directly conflicts with Legal's stance that it can't be guaranteed in writing—they're really two sides of the same contradiction. The primary blocker is this guarantee versus best effort tension between Legal and the PM/CS teams. Beyond that, I'm seeing some gaps that need clarification: what's the fallback when an express order goes out but no driver shows up within 15 minutes, how exactly do we measure that 2-hour window from order placement or pickup, and what's the policy on partial deliveries.

There are also edge cases around driver cancellations—if a replacement driver also cancels, what happens then—and the warehouse proximity rule is vague about whether "within 10km" means the nearest warehouse or any warehouse. Given all these unresolved contradictions and ambiguities, my confidence here is around 50% or lower.

Legal explicitly states: 'We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing — this is a best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee. The refund is a goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These positions are mutually exclusive. You cannot prominently display a 'guarantee' while legally disclaiming it as best-effort. This affects: (1) all customer-facing copy and UI, (2) whether refunds are automated obligations or discretionary, (3) terms of service language, (4) potential regulatory/consumer-protection exposure.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Customer Success","Legal","Customers","Marketing"],"options":["A: Adopt Legal's position — label as 'best effort' with goodwill refund.
